description: Evaluation benchmarks using real open-source repositories. Each eval clones a specific release tag, runs the full audit flow, and compares against expected findings.

human_verification_protocol.description: Manual post-eval protocol (not automated). After running an eval, a human reviewer verifies: (1) each key_finding maps to real evidence in the repo, (2) no critical findings were missed, (3) anti-pattern identification is accurate. Record verification status in the ground_truth_verified field of each eval's expectations.
verification_checklist: Each key_finding has a corresponding file/config in the repo
Score is within tolerance of independent manual scoring
No false positives in key_findings (finding references something that does not exist)

execution.report_path_note: report_path values are OUTPUT destinations, not pre-existing files. Reports are generated when evals are run. Paths under examples/ serve as golden references once generated.

evals.name: OpenClaw (Dec 2025 baseline)
evals.purpose: Regression baseline — harness state before date-based versioning

key_findings: AGENTS.md quality — check line count and progressive disclosure
CI pipeline exists and blocks on failure

evals.name: OpenClaw (Jan 2026)
evals.purpose: Regression tracking — time-series point 2, track harness maturity evolution

evals.name: OpenClaw (Feb 2026)
evals.purpose: Regression tracking — time-series point 3, track harness maturity evolution

evals.name: OpenClaw (Mar 2026 — golden reference)
evals.purpose: Regression golden reference — most recent state, primary benchmark for skill regression testing

key_findings: AGENTS.md exists — check conciseness against dynamic threshold
CI pipeline blocks on failure with multiple jobs
7.4 Environment recovery via docker-compose or init scripts
quick_mode_expectations.notes: Quick Audit score may differ from Full Audit due to reduced item coverage. Scores within ±10 of Full Audit score are expected.

evals.name: OpenCode
evals.purpose: Large TS monorepo (Bun + Turbo) — calibration for high-velocity development projects

key_findings: 32 GitHub Actions workflows — exemplary CI pipeline (2.1 PASS)
No root docs/ directory; documentation at non-standard paths packages/docs/ and packages/web/src/content/docs/ (1.2 PARTIAL)
No ARCHITECTURE.md; CONTRIBUTING.md lacks domain boundaries and dependency direction (1.3 FAIL)
ESLint only in sdks/vscode/, core workspace packages have no linter; CI has no lint step (2.2 FAIL/PARTIAL)
Prettier configured but no CI format check (2.3 PARTIAL)
No eslint-plugin-boundaries or explicit dependency direction enforcement (2.5 FAIL)
TypeScript strict mode + typecheck.yml in CI (2.4 PASS)
Effect framework is niche and AI-unfriendly, reducing tech composability (5.4 PARTIAL)
rmslop command exists but is manual, not automated via lint rules (6.4 PARTIAL)
flake.nix + Dockerfile provide strong environment recovery (7.4 PASS)
Git tag releases enable ad-hoc rollback but no documented playbook (8.3 PARTIAL)
must_identify_anti_patterns: Trust the agent (no dependency direction enforcement in 20+ package monorepo)
Mislabeled lint script (packages/opencode lint runs bun test --coverage)

evals.name: OpenFang
evals.purpose: Rust multi-crate system — validates scoring for safety-critical infrastructure projects

key_findings: Rust cargo workspace with multiple crates
Clippy and cargo fmt enforcement in CI
CLAUDE.md exists but lacks cross-references to docs/ (1.1 PARTIAL)
Progressive disclosure chain broken at agent entry point (1.4 PARTIAL)
Project-specific conventions documented but not mechanically enforced (2.7 PARTIAL)
No AI-specific slop detection rules (6.4 FAIL, distinct from linter in 2.2)
xtask crate is empty stub (7.1 FAIL)
must_identify_anti_patterns: Trust the agent (partial — dependency direction not enforced)

evals.name: Cherry Studio
evals.purpose: Electron desktop app — validates desktop-app profile and dual agent instruction file handling

evals.name: Paperclip
evals.purpose: Full-stack orchestration platform (Node + React) — validates fullstack profile scoring

key_findings: Testing strategy across frontend and backend

time_series_analysis.description: OpenClaw evals 1a-1d form a time series showing harness maturity evolution over 4 months. After running all four audits, compare scores and findings to validate that the skill detects incremental improvements.
time_series_analysis.expected_trend: generally upward or stable trend; investigate any large drops (>10 points) for regression causes; specific dimension improvements should be identifiable at each checkpoint
